name: SQUAMOUS CELL CARCINOMA OF KIDNEY

demographics.Frequency: 5-10% of all urothelial tumors; 1% of renal neoplasms; 2nd most common
demographics.Associated with: chronic irritation of urothelium by renal infection + calculi (25-60%)

dominant_finding: thickening of pelvicaliceal wall (with superficial spread over large areas)
discriminating_features: stricture that may simulate extrinsic cause

general: stricture that may simulate extrinsic cause
CT: thickening of pelvicaliceal wall (with superficial spread over large areas)

clinical.Prognosis: worse than TCC ← early metastases; 33% 1-year survival rate
